prevalence.source: ECCO and European IBD registry syntheses
prevalence.trend: stable to increasing with improved survival and diagnosis

burden.dalyPerCapita: moderate due to chronic disability and complications

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical symptoms plus endoscopy with biopsy; imaging for extent; exclude infections

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Hundreds of thousands across the EU
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with access to biologics and specialist IBD care

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Colonoscopy and imaging widely available; standardized IBD pathways
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Steroids for flares, immunomodulators, biologics and small molecules; surgery for complications
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Long-term adherence and monitoring of biologic therapy
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong IBD centers and broad access to advanced therapies
